paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Then sang Moses and the children of Israel this song unto Jehovah, and spake, saying, I will sing unto Jehovah, for he hath triumphed gloriously: The horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Jehovah is my strength and song, And he is become my salvation: This is my God, and I will praise him; My father`s God, and I will exalt him.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Jehovah is a man of war: Jehovah is his name.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Pharaoh`s chariots and his host hath he cast into the sea; And his chosen captains are sunk in the Red Sea.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The deeps cover them: They went down into the depths like a stone.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thy right hand, O Jehovah, is glorious in power, Thy right hand, O Jehovah, dasheth in pieces the enemy.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And in the greatness of thine excellency thou       overthrowest them that rise up against thee: Thou sendest       forth thy wrath, it consumeth them as stubble.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And with the blast of thy nostrils the waters were piled       up, The floods stood upright as a heap; The deeps were congealed in the heart of the sea.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The enemy said, I will pursue, I will overtake, I will       divide the spoil; My desire shall be satisfied upon them; I will draw my sword, my hand shall destroy them.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thou didst blow with thy wind, the sea covered them: They sank as lead in the mighty waters.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Who is like unto thee, O Jehovah, among the gods? Who is like thee, glorious in holiness, Fearful in praises, doing wonders?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thou stretchedst out thy right hand, The earth swallowed them.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thou in thy lovingkindness hast led the people that thou       hast redeemed: Thou hast guided them in thy strength to thy holy habitation.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The peoples have heard, they tremble: Pangs have taken hold on the inhabitants of Philistia.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Then were the chiefs of Edom dismayed; The mighty men of Moab, trembling taketh hold upon them: All the inhabitants of Canaan are melted away.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Terror and dread falleth upon them; By the greatness of thine arm they are as still as a stone; Till thy people pass over, O Jehovah, Till the people pass over that thou hast purchased.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thou wilt bring them in, and plant them in the mountain       of thine inheritance, The place, O Jehovah, which thou hast made for thee to dwell in, The sanctuary, O Lord, which thy hands have established.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Jehovah shall reign for ever and ever.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: For the horses of Pharaoh went in with his chariots and with his horsemen into the sea, and Jehovah brought back the waters of the sea upon them; but the children of Israel walked on dry land in the midst of the sea.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Miriam the prophetess, the sister of Aaron, took a timbrel in her hand; and all the women went out after her with timbrels and with dances.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Miriam answered them, Sing ye to Jehovah, for he hath triumphed gloriously; The horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses led Israel onward from the Red Sea, and they went out into the wilderness of Shur; and they went three days in the wilderness, and found no water.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And when they came to Marah, they could not drink of the waters of Marah, for they were bitter: therefore the name of it was called Marah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the people murmured against Moses, saying, What shall we drink?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: An he cried unto Jehovah; And Jehovah showed him a tree, and he cast it into the waters, and the waters were made sweet. There he made for them a statute and an ordinance, and there he proved them;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and he said, If thou wilt diligently hearken to the voice of Jehovah thy God, and wilt do that which is right in his eyes, and wilt give ear to his commandments, and keep all his statutes, I will put none of the diseases upon thee, which I have put upon the Egyptians: for I am Jehovah that healeth thee.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they came to Elim, where were twelve springs of water, and threescore and ten palm-trees: and they encamped there by the waters.
